behaviors.description: The wait time between retries doubles with each consecutive failure, up to a configured maximum.
behaviors.justification: Behavior quote gate: "wait time between retries doubles ... up to a configured maximum" is deterministic backoff math and capping logic. No module handoff is required to trigger the failure; a single backoff function/state object test can fail on real arithmetic/cap bugs. Rejected higher levels: no behavior text requires real HTTP/OS/subprocess (System) or AI output (Agentic/Workflow).
behaviors.test_description: Test a pure backoff calculator with failure counts 1..N and a max cap; assert exponential progression and cap enforcement.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The described test uses one local function/object and no I/O.

behaviors.description: The retry logic correctly identifies a rate-limit response and computes the appropriate wait time before the next attempt.
behaviors.justification: Behavior quote gate: "identifies a rate-limit response and computes the appropriate wait time" implies boundary interaction between response classification and retry/backoff modules. A real failure can occur in handoff (e.g., status/header mapping) even if each function passes unit tests. Integration brake checks: no required real OS/network/subprocess in the behavior text, so not System; no AI dependence, so not Agentic/Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Wire retry policy + response parser/classifier with a stub transport response (e.g., 429 plus retry metadata) and assert the computed next-delay used by retry controller.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Requires module wiring; no real external infra required.

behaviors.description: After the wait, the client actually resends the original request and returns the successful result to the caller transparently.
behaviors.justification: Behavior quote gate: "after the wait, the client actually resends the original request and returns the successful result" requires interaction among timer/backoff, request replay, transport adapter, and result propagation. Unit tests on isolated functions can miss replay/wiring bugs. Rejected System: behavior does not require real network behavior; stub transport plus controlled clock can expose this failure.
behaviors.test_description: Use fake timers and a stub transport that fails once with rate-limit then succeeds; assert second send contains original request payload and `call()` resolves to the success value.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: This test genuinely needs multi-module orchestration but not real HTTP.

behaviors.description: After the configured maximum number of retries, the client returns a typed `QuotaExhaustedError` rather than a generic error.
behaviors.justification: Behavior quote gate: "after the configured maximum number of retries" + "returns a typed `QuotaExhaustedError`" spans retry-loop state and terminal error mapping. Real failures often occur at the boundary where retry exhaustion is converted to public API error type. Rejected System: no behavior text requires real OS/network effects; repeated stubbed failures are sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Configure max retries, force transport to return rate-limit on every attempt, run `call()`, and assert rejection is `QuotaExhaustedError` (type and key fields), not generic error.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Needs retry orchestrator + error translation together.

behaviors.description: The full retry chain — initial call, backoff retries, quota tracking — terminates correctly and does not loop indefinitely before surfacing the error.
behaviors.justification: Behavior quote gate: "full retry chain — initial call, backoff retries, quota tracking" is explicitly cross-module orchestration/state handoff. The failure boundary is loop control across components, not one function. Rejected System: phrase does not require real HTTP/OS primitives; fake timers plus stub transport can detect non-termination or excessive attempts.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end in-process retry chain with controlled clock and always-rate-limited responses; assert bounded number of attempts, terminal error emitted, and no further scheduled retries.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Level matches because termination bugs are in orchestration across modules.

behaviors.description: Requests issued concurrently from multiple async callers in the same process all draw from the same shared quota bucket.
behaviors.justification: Behavior quote gate: "concurrently from multiple async callers in the same process" + "same shared quota bucket" requires shared-state coordination across caller-facing API and quota manager. Unit tests on a single function miss contention/race ordering bugs. Integration brake: no real OS/network requirement is stated; async in-process concurrency is enough. No AI dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Fire many concurrent `call()` invocations against one client instance with deterministic stub responses; assert aggregate token/attempt accounting is global (shared) rather than per-caller.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Needs concurrent module interaction in one process.

behaviors.description: When multiple requests are waiting to be sent, they are dispatched in the order they were originally submitted.
behaviors.justification: Behavior quote gate: "multiple requests are waiting" and "dispatched in the order ... submitted" is queueing/scheduler behavior across async boundaries. Real failure is at handoff between enqueue, wakeup, and dispatcher, not isolated logic only. Rejected System: no behavior text requires real network or OS events; in-process queue + stub sender can expose reorder bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Queue several blocked requests with known submission order, release capacity gradually, and assert dispatch/send order is FIFO under async scheduling.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Requires orchestrated queue and dispatcher modules; no external infra.
